frozen_components: LLMs (GPT-4o-mini, Ministral-8B, Llama-8B, DeepSeek-8B, DeepSeek-32B, DeepSeek-V3)
symbolic solvers (Pyke, Clingo for ASP, Prover9 for NLTK/FOL)
architecture.summary: A neurosymbolic system that translates natural-language logical reasoning problems into formal intermediate languages using in-context learning with LLMs, then solves the translated problems with symbolic reasoners (Pyke, ASP, NLTK/Prover9, or FOL). The LLM acts as a translator from natural language to formal syntax; the symbolic engine provides faithful reasoning. Information flows: natural language problem → LLM (with ICL examples) → formal language representation → symbolic solver → solution.

biblio.title: Intermediate Languages Matter: Formal Choice Drives Neurosymbolic LLM Reasoning

claims.statement: The choice of formal language affects both the syntactic and the semantic reasoning capability of LLMs in neurosymbolic reasoning.
First-order logic (FOL) outperforms logic programming languages (Pyke and ASP) on average overall-accuracy.

claims.dataset: ProntoQA and ProofWriter averaged across all formal languages and LLMs
claims.statement: Context-aware encodings (Text) improve LLM reasoning performance compared to no context (No-C.), achieving 58.42% overall-accuracy versus 45.49%.
Adding comments or markdown syntax to in-context-learning examples yields no systematic benefit.
For the 8B parameter model class (Ministral-8B), FOL achieves 100.0% overall-accuracy on ProntoQA with context and comments, compared to 48.80% for standard prompting baseline.

Logic-LM* and LINC* baselines perform poorly (2.20% and 6.20% overall-accuracy on ProntoQA, respectively) due to susceptibility to small syntax errors such as markdown formatting and line enumeration.
ASP has execution-rate ranging from 62% to 95% and maintains overall-accuracy above 30%, while Pyke has most ablation scenarios below 70% overall-accuracy.

Common LLM errors across formal languages include token generation cutoff, Pyke formatting issues (missing line breaks), ASP negation confusion (strong vs. default negation), and NLTK/FOL parenthesis and arity errors.
